name: pure-protobuf

metadata.name: pure-protobuf
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Protocol Buffers using Python type annotations

documentation: # `pure-protobuf` [![GitHub Workflow Status](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/eigenein/protobuf/check.yml?label=checks&logo=github)](https://github.com/eigenein/protobuf/actions/workflows/check.yml) [![Code coverage](https://codecov.io/gh/eigenein/protobuf/branch/master/graph/badge.svg?token=bJarwbLlY7)](https://codecov.io/gh/eigenein/protobuf) [![PyPI - Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/pure-protobuf.svg)](https://pypistats.org/packages/pure-protobuf) [![PyPI – Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pure-protobuf.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/pure-protobuf/#history) [![PyPI – Python](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/pure-protobuf.svg?logo=python&logoColor=yellow)](https://pypi.org/project/pure-protobuf/#files) [![License](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/pure-protobuf.svg)](https://github.com/eigenein/protobuf/blob/master/LICENSE) Wow! Such annotated! Very buffers! ## Documentation ## Quick examples ### `.proto` definition It's not needed for `pure-protobuf`, but for the sake of an example, let's consider the following definition: ```protobuf syntax = "proto3"; message SearchRequest { string query = 1; int32 page_number = 2; int32 result_per_page = 3; } ``` And here's the same via `pure-protobuf`: ### With [dataclasses](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html) ```python title="dataclass_example.py" from dataclasses import dataclass from io import BytesIO from pure_protobuf.annotations import Field from pure_protobuf.message import BaseMessage from typing_extensions import Annotated @dataclass class SearchRequest(BaseMessage): query: Annotated[str, Field(1)] = "" page_number: Annotated[int, Field(2)] = 0 result_per_page: Annotated[int, Field(3)] = 0 request = SearchRequest(query="hello", page_number=1, result_per_page=10) buffer = bytes(request) assert buffer == b"\x0A\x05hello\x10\x01\x18\x0A" assert SearchRequest.read_from(BytesIO(buffer)) == request ``` ### With [`pydantic`](https://docs.pydantic.dev/) ```python title="pydantic_example.py" from io import BytesIO from pure_protobuf.annotations import Field from pure_protobuf.message import BaseMessage from pydantic import BaseModel from typing_extensions import Annotated class SearchRequest(BaseMessage, BaseModel): query: Annotated[str, Field(1)] = "" page_number: Annotated[int, Field(2)] = 0 result_per_page: Annotated[int, Field(3)] = 0 request = SearchRequest(query="hello", page_number=1, result_per_page=10) buffer = bytes(request) assert buffer == b"\x0A\x05hello\x10\x01\x18\x0A" assert SearchRequest.read_from(BytesIO(buffer)) == request ```

modules.name: pure_protobuf.io.varint
documentation:
Reading and writing varints and the derivative types.

See Also:
    - https://developers.google.com/protocol-buffers/docs/encoding#varints

classes.name: pure_protobuf.io.varint.ReadUnsignedVarint
documentation: Reads unsigned varint from the stream.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.io.varint.WriteUnsignedVarint
documentation: Writes unsigned varint to the stream.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.io.varint.ReadZigZagVarint
documentation:
Reads a ZigZag-encoded varint.

See Also:
    - https://stackoverflow.com/a/2211086/359730.

documentation: Writes a ZigZag-encoded varint.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.io.varint.ReadTwosComplimentVarint

Reads a two's compliment varint.

documentation:
Writes a two's compliment varint.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.io.wrappers.ReadLengthDelimited

methods.name: pure_protobuf.io.wrappers.ReadLengthDelimited.__init__
documentation: noinspection PyProtocol

Make the inner reader length-delimited.

This reader wraps the inner reader so that it would first read a byte string,
and then call the inner reader on that byte string.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.interfaces.write.Write
documentation: Serializes and writes the value into the stream.

classes.name: pure_protobuf._accumulators.AccumulateLastOneWins
documentation:
The simplest accumulator which keeps only the last seen item.

Typical use case is fields of primitive scalar types.

See Also:
    - https://developers.google.com/protocol-buffers/docs/encoding#last-one-wins

methods.name: pure_protobuf._accumulators.AccumulateAppend.__call__
documentation: Append all items from the `other` into the accumulator.

methods.name: pure_protobuf._accumulators.AccumulateMessages.__init__
documentation: noinspection PyProtocol

methods.name: pure_protobuf._accumulators.AccumulateMessages.__call__
documentation:
Merge the two messages into the left one.

Notes:
    - **Never** reuse `lhs` or `rhs` afterwards, consider them consumed by the method.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.interfaces._repr.Repr
documentation:
Provides default implementations for `str(self)` and `repr(self)` to make
debugging easier.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.interfaces.accumulate.Accumulate
documentation:
Accumulates the value in the accumulator (also known as «fold» or «aggregate»).
Implementation of the protocol defines how a record value from the input buffer
should be «merged» into a corresponding attribute.

variables.name: pure_protobuf.interfaces._vars.FieldT
documentation: Message field's type.

variables.name: pure_protobuf.interfaces._vars.RecordT
documentation: Field's record type. May be the field's type itself, or a single item out of it.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.interfaces.merge.Merge
documentation: Defines how to merge two values of the same field.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.interfaces._skip.Skip
documentation:
Knows how to skip a value. It's equivalent to `Read`, but is supposed to be a little
more efficient because it doesn't need to actually parse the field data.

methods.name: pure_protobuf.interfaces._skip.SkipNoOperation.__call__
documentation: Do nothing. This is useful for the deprecated groups feature.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.exceptions.IncorrectWireTypeError

classes.name: pure_protobuf.exceptions.UnexpectedWireTypeError
documentation: The wire type is correct, but a different one was expected.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.exceptions.IncorrectAnnotationError
documentation: Something wrong with the attribute annotation.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.exceptions.UnsupportedAnnotationError
documentation: The type annotation is not supported.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.exceptions.IncorrectValueError
documentation: Something's wrong with the field value.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.interfaces.read.Read
documentation: Deserializes a value from the stream and yields it as an iterator.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.annotations.Field

methods.name: pure_protobuf.annotations.Field._from_annotated_args
documentation: Extract itself from the `Annotated[_, *args]` type hint, if present.

documentation:
Specifies the field's number.

See also:
    - https://developers.google.com/protocol-buffers/docs/proto3#assigning_field_numbers

class_variables.name: pure_protobuf.annotations.Field.packed
documentation: Specifies whether the field should be packed in its serialized representation.

documentation: Specifies a one-of group for this field.
Annotates a Protocol Buffers field.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.message.BaseMessage

methods.name: pure_protobuf.message.BaseMessage.read_from
documentation: Read a message from the file.

methods.name: pure_protobuf.message.BaseMessage.loads
documentation:
Read a message from the buffer.

This is functionally the same as calling `read_from(BytesIO(buffer))`.

methods.name: pure_protobuf.message.BaseMessage.write_to
documentation: Write the message to the file.

methods.name: pure_protobuf.message.BaseMessage.__bytes__
documentation:
Convert the message to a bytestring.

This is functionally the same as calling `dumps()` or `write_to(BytesIO(…))`.

methods.name: pure_protobuf.message.BaseMessage.dumps
documentation:
Convert the message to a bytestring.

This is functionally the same as calling `bytes(message)` or `write_to(BytesIO(…))`.

class_variables.name: pure_protobuf.message.BaseMessage.__PROTOBUF_SKIP__
documentation: Defines how to skip a field of the given wire type.
Base message class, inherit from it to define a specific message.

modules.name: pure_protobuf.well_known
documentation: Well-known types.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.well_known._TimeSpan
documentation: Base class to represent timespan as whole seconds plus its nanoseconds part.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.well_known.Timestamp

methods.name: pure_protobuf.well_known.Timestamp.from_datetime
documentation: Convert the `datetime` to `Timestamp`.

methods.name: pure_protobuf.well_known.Timestamp.into_datetime
documentation: Convert to `datetime`.

Implements the [`Timestamp`](https://protobuf.dev/reference/protobuf/google.protobuf/#timestamp)
well-known type and supports conversion from and to
[`datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime-objects).

classes.name: pure_protobuf.well_known.Duration

methods.name: pure_protobuf.well_known.Duration.from_timedelta
documentation: Convert the `timedelta` into `Duration`.

methods.name: pure_protobuf.well_known.Duration.into_timedelta
documentation: Convert into `timedelta`.

Implements the [`#!protobuf Duration`](https://developers.google.com/protocol-buffers/docs/reference/google.protobuf#duration)
well-known type.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.well_known.Any_

methods.name: pure_protobuf.well_known.Any_.from_message
documentation: Convert the message into its `Any_` representation.

methods.name: pure_protobuf.well_known.Any_.into_message
documentation:
Reconstructs a message from the current `Any_` representation.

Args:
    locals_: forwarded to the `__import__` call
    globals_: forwarded to the `__import__` call

See Also:
    - https://developers.google.com/protocol-buffers/docs/proto3#any

Well-known `Any` type.

See Also:
    - https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/blob/master/src/google/protobuf/any.proto.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.one_of.OneOf

methods.name: pure_protobuf.one_of.OneOf.__init__
documentation:
Define a one-of group of fields.

A [`Field`][pure_protobuf.annotations.Field] then should be assigned to the group
via the [`one_of`][pure_protobuf.annotations.Field.one_of] parameter.

methods.name: pure_protobuf.one_of.OneOf.which_one_of_getter
documentation: Construct a getter which returns which attribute is actually set.

methods.name: pure_protobuf.one_of.OneOf._which_one_of
documentation: Return which of the attributes is actually set.

See Also:
    - https://developers.google.com/protocol-buffers/docs/proto3#oneof.

classes.name: pure_protobuf._mergers.MergeLastOneWins
documentation:
Always returns the right-hand side.

See Also:
    - https://developers.google.com/protocol-buffers/docs/encoding#last-one-wins

methods.name: pure_protobuf._mergers.MergeMessages.__init__
documentation: noinspection PyProtocol

methods.name: pure_protobuf._mergers.MergeMessages.__call__
documentation:
Merge the two messages.

Returns:
    Merged message: it'll be either possibly updated `lhs` or non-updated `rhs`.

Notes:
    - **Never** reuse either `lhs` or `rhs` after calling this method, consider
    them consumed.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.descriptors._field._FieldDescriptor

methods.name: pure_protobuf.descriptors._field._FieldDescriptor.from_attribute
documentation:
Construct a descriptor from the attribute's type hint.

Returns:
    Field's descriptor, or `None` if skipped.

class_variables.name: pure_protobuf.descriptors._field._FieldDescriptor.number
documentation:
Field's number.

See Also:
    - https://developers.google.com/protocol-buffers/docs/proto3#assigning_field_numbers

class_variables.name: pure_protobuf.descriptors._field._FieldDescriptor.one_of
documentation:
See Also:
    - https://developers.google.com/protocol-buffers/docs/proto3#oneof

Describes how the field should be read and written.

Notes:
    - It's not a «real» Python descriptor, just didn't come up with a better name.
    - It gets converted from `RecordDescriptor`.

functions.name: pure_protobuf.helpers._typing.extract_repeated
documentation: Extract a possible repeated flag.

functions.name: pure_protobuf.helpers._typing.extract_optional
documentation: Extract a possible optional flag.

variables.name: pure_protobuf.helpers._typing.NoneType
documentation: type: ignore

classes.name: pure_protobuf.helpers._typing.Sentinel
documentation: Sentinel object used for defaults.

functions.name: pure_protobuf.helpers.datetime.split_seconds
documentation: Split seconds into whole seconds and nanoseconds.

functions.name: pure_protobuf.helpers.datetime.unsplit_seconds
documentation: Merge whole seconds and nanoseconds back to normal seconds.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.descriptors.record.RecordDescriptor

methods.name: pure_protobuf.descriptors.record.RecordDescriptor._from_inner_type_hint
documentation:
Construct a descriptor from the inner type hint.

Examples:
    - For `Annotated[int, Field[1]]` the inner hint is `int`.
    - For `Annotated[List[int], Field[1]]` it's also `int`
      since the `List` has already been extracted by `Field._from_annotated_args`.

Args:
    message_type: message type which contains the attribute being described
    inner_hint: the attribute's own type hint

class_variables.name: pure_protobuf.descriptors.record.RecordDescriptor.wire_type
documentation: Field's record [wire type](https://developers.google.com/protocol-buffers/docs/encoding#structure).

class_variables.name: pure_protobuf.descriptors.record.RecordDescriptor.write
documentation: Write a complete value to the stream, altogether with its tag.

class_variables.name: pure_protobuf.descriptors.record.RecordDescriptor.read
documentation:
Read a record from the stream.

This behaves differently from the [`write`][pure_protobuf.descriptors.record.RecordDescriptor.write],
because it's only supposed to read a single record from the stream
(it may be, for example, just one item of a packed repeated field).
Also, it assumes that the tag has already been read by [`BaseMessage`][base-message].

class_variables.name: pure_protobuf.descriptors.record.RecordDescriptor.accumulate
documentation:
Accumulate a value from the stream into an existing field value.
It follows the `read` to decide which value should be assigned to the attribute.

class_variables.name: pure_protobuf.descriptors.record.RecordDescriptor.merge
documentation: Merge two values of the same field from different messages. Only called in a message merger.

class_variables.name: pure_protobuf.descriptors.record.RecordDescriptor.__PREDEFINED__
documentation: Pre-defined descriptors for primitive types.
Describes how records should be read, written, accumulated, and merged. Not a «real» Python descriptor.

modules.name: pure_protobuf.helpers.io
documentation: Input/output helpers.

functions.name: pure_protobuf.helpers.io.read_byte_checked
documentation:
Read one byte.

Raises:
    EOFError: the stream has ended

functions.name: pure_protobuf.helpers.io.read_checked
documentation:
Read the specified number of bytes.

Raises:
    EOFError: the stream has ended

modules.name: pure_protobuf.helpers._dataclasses
documentation: Backwards compatibility with Python 3.9.

classes.name: pure_protobuf.helpers.itertools.ReadCallback
documentation: Convert a reader, which returns a singular scalar value, into an iterable reader.
